findings.title: Null sentinel forces double-narrowing at every call site
findings.detail: networkMismatch is typed WalletNetworkResult | null, but WalletNetworkResult already encodes 'no mismatch' as { match: true }. Callers must now write `networkMismatch && !networkMismatch.match` (three terms) to reach the mismatch payload. Using `WalletNetworkResult` everywhere with a default of `{ match: true }` would eliminate null from the surface API entirely. The defaultContextValue and every consumer would only need `if (!networkMismatch.match)` to get to the typed payload. The current shape is not wrong, but it is noisier than necessary given that the type already has a clean sentinel value.
findings.suggestion: Change the type to `WalletNetworkResult` (no null), initialize state to `{ match: true }`, and clear the mismatch on disconnect via `setNetworkMismatch({ match: true })`. Remove the null checks from consumers — the discriminant alone is sufficient.

findings.title: UI description branching is not exhaustively checked by TS
findings.detail: The description is computed via nested ternaries on `actualIsTestnet` (boolean) and `expected === 'mainnet'` (string equality). TypeScript cannot enforce that all AppNetwork values produce a string — if 'preview' were renamed or a fourth variant added, the else branch would silently reuse the wrong copy. The logic is correct today but TS provides no exhaustiveness guarantee here.
findings.suggestion: Restructure as an explicit record lookup or a switch over `expected` so the compiler can flag unhandled variants: `const copy: Record<AppNetwork, string> = { mainnet: '...', preprod: '...', preview: '...' }; const description = copy[networkMismatch.expected]`. The `actualIsTestnet` distinction for the mainnet-vs-testnet case can still live inside each entry.

findings.title: AppNetwork duplicates the existing CardanoNetwork type
findings.detail: src/lib/constants.ts already exports `CardanoNetwork = 'mainnet' | 'preprod' | 'preview'` derived from EXPLORER_URLS. AppNetwork in wallet-network.ts is an identical string union declared independently. If EXPLORER_URLS ever gains a new key, AppNetwork stays out of sync silently.
findings.suggestion: Import and re-export `CardanoNetwork` from constants.ts as the parameter type for `checkWalletNetwork`, or at minimum add a compile-time equality assertion: `type _AssertSame = AppNetwork extends CardanoNetwork ? CardanoNetwork extends AppNetwork ? true : never : never`.

findings.title: NetworkCapableWallet could be narrowed to 0 | 1 return type
findings.detail: getNetworkId is declared as returning Promise<number>. CIP-30 specifies only 0 or 1. The comparison `networkId === expectedId` works, but the subsequent `actualIsTestnet: networkId === 0` could silently pass for any non-1 integer (e.g., 2, 99) from a non-compliant or mocked wallet. A runtime assertion or a narrower interface (0 | 1) would make the assumption explicit.
findings.suggestion: Optionally narrow with a runtime check: `if (networkId !== 0 && networkId !== 1) throw new Error(...)` before the comparison. Even without that, changing the interface to `getNetworkId: () => Promise<0 | 1>` documents the CIP-30 contract and lets callers see what the function expects.

residual_risks.description: Race between the network-check effect and the auto-auth effect. Both depend on `connected` but are separate useEffects with no ordering guarantee. If auto-auth fires before the network check resolves, the `networkMismatch && !networkMismatch.match` guard in auto-auth reads stale state (null on first render). The belt-and-braces check inside authenticate() partially covers this, but a fast wallet could still call signData() once before the check effect sets state.

env.NEXT_PUBLIC_CARDANO_NETWORK is typed by t3-oss as the Zod-inferred output of z.enum(['mainnet','preprod','preview']).default('preprod'). The inferred type is 'mainnet' | 'preprod' | 'preview', which is structurally compatible with both AppNetwork and CardanoNetwork — but this relies on Zod preserving the literal union rather than widening to string. If the env schema is ever changed (e.g., to z.string()), the call site checkWalletNetwork(wallet, env.NEXT_PUBLIC_CARDANO_NETWORK) will break at the type level, not silently at runtime. This is acceptable but worth knowing.

testing_gaps.description: checkWalletNetwork has no tests. The function is pure enough to unit-test directly — mock getNetworkId to return 0 or 1 and assert the result shape for all three expected AppNetwork values. The mismatch between preprod and preview (both return 0) is particularly worth testing because it produces a { match: true } result even though the app is on a different testnet, which may surprise users.

There is no coverage of the race path described in RR-01 — specifically, what happens when connected becomes true and the auto-auth effect runs synchronously before the async network check resolves and sets state. A test that mocks both effects firing on the same tick would confirm the belt-and-braces check in authenticate() actually covers the gap.
